items.title: Your Social Security check could go up by $74 a month next year
items.description: Social Security’s cost-of-living-adjustment could be 3.8% in 2027, as inflation shows signs of cooling.

items.title: How a homegrown Chinese chip maker became the memory industry’s biggest wild card
items.description: As ChangXin Memory Technologies gears up for a breakout public listing, the chip maker is eyeing the turf held by Micron, SK Hynix and Samsung.

items.title: Forget gasoline: Why the price surge in this under-the-radar fuel is the real threat to the U.S. economy
items.description: As investors fret about gasoline, diesel prices have been rising and are threatening to cause even more economic destruction.

items.title: This may be the real reason USDA made a huge revision to its beef-export sales data
items.description: It’s ‘like driving one more nail into the proverbial coffin,’ says a rancher about the recent deterioration in U.S. agriculture data

items.title: Will the Fed hike interest rates this month?
items.description: Kevin Warsh’s testimony to Congress “offered some heat but not a lot of new light” on the outlook for interest rates, one economist said.

items.title: Meet Kimi K3, the newest Chinese AI model haunting Silicon Valley
items.description: Moonshot AI’s new open model is catching up to Anthropic and OpenAI, raising questions about the future of the AI race.

items.title: The World Cup–winning side will make $50 million — and the IRS gets a cut
items.description: “It doesn’t make a difference who wins the game. The IRS will get a piece.”

items.title: SpaceX postponed a crucial launch — and its stock went on to close below IPO price for second day
items.description: Shares in SpaceX declined about 4% in premarket trading on Friday after aborted mission.

items.title: The average stock is having a moment as semiconductors struggle. It’s a sign of a healthy market.
items.description: It has been a good week to be average on Wall Street.

items.title: 19 (mostly) tech stocks that have fallen at least 25% in July
items.description: Seven of the worst performers for the month were still up by triple digits for 2026.
